channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate advances nomination of Kash Patel, Trump's pick for FBI director
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday along party lines to advance the nomination of Kash Patel, President Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, setting up a final vote on the Trump ally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet Dale E. Ho, the second-year judge weighing the future of NYC Mayor Eric Adams' corruption case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer asked, top officials in President Donald Trump's Justice Department agreed, and seven prosecutors resigned in protest. Now, it’s up to Dale E. Ho, a second-year federal judge in Manhattan, to decide if Adams' corruption case goes away.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Howard Lutnick as commerce secretary, a key role for Trump's trade agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed wealthy financier Howard Lutnick as commerce secretary Tuesday, putting in place a staunch supporter of President Donald Trump's hardline trade polices.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving improper Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scott Stringer: I hope Hochul suspends NYC Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: Stringer, who hopes to unseat Adams in June's Democratic primary for mayor, believes "we have to resolve this, one way or another.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: NY Gov Hochul weighs decision to remove Mayor Adams
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul and officials discuss the “path forward" for Mayor Eric Adams after a slew of City Hall resignations in the wake of the DOJ dropping charges against him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump issues warning about wasteful spending, orders 'radical transparency' amid DOGE probes, revelations
item.description: In a memo published Tuesday, President Trump firmly instructed government agency leaders to start being "radically transparent" about their expenditures.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Zelensky 'should have never started' war with Russia
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday appeared to blame Ukraine's leaders for the three-year war with Russia, arguing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "should have never started it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kennedy says panel will examine childhood vaccine schedule after promising not to change it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — To earn the vote he needed to become the nation's top health official, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. made a special promise to a U.S. senator: He would not change the nation's current vaccination schedule. But on Tuesday, speaking for the first time to thousands of U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin reveals list of cartels and gangs to be designated terrorist organizations
item.description: The Trump administration sent a list of half a dozen drug cartels to Congress last week that it plans to designate as foreign terrorist organizations, Fox News confirmed on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louisiana surgeon general wants to 'depoliticize medicine' by ending statewide mass vaccinations
item.description: After directing staff at the Louisiana Department of Health to halt all mass vaccination programs, Louisiana Surgeon General Dr. Ralph Abraham speaks to Fox News Digital about the criticisms his move has faced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York City Mayor Eric Adams will face judge who will decide whether to dismiss his criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams will face the federal judge on Wednesday who will decide whether to grant the Justice Department's request to dismiss corruption charges against him after lawyers explain the abrupt change in position just weeks before an April trial. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to study how to expand IVF and calls for 'radical transparency' from government
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order to study how to expand access to in vitro fertilization and make it more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Hochul heads to NYC for meetings after 4 deputy mayors resign
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul is holding meetings on Tuesday in New York City as calls grow for Mayor Eric Adams to resign from his position. She’s set to meet with City Comptroller Brad Lander, City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams, Rev.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: 35% of people will never support Trump, DOGE
item.description: "I want this; 80% of the American people are with me. The Trump haters don't want it," O'Reilly said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The firings come just weeks after an American Airlines flight collided mid-air with a military Black Hawk helicopter shortly before landing at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Europe tries to muscle up as U.S. plots mediation to help end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: European leaders are taking steps to answer assertive U.S. efforts to end Russia’s war against Ukraine. France on Sunday announced a meeting of continental leaders and Baltic countries called for new steps to strengthen Kyiv’s hand in any future talks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: PIX on Politics Panel breaks down Adams-DOJ controversy
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a politically charged week for Mayor Eric Adams after the Department of Justice ordered prosecutors to drop a federal corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Skyrocketing fees: St. Pete condo owners say they never asked for bailout, only relief
item.description: Condo owners are waiting for answers on whether lawmakers will do anything to ease concerns over rising fees.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
